parcel = parcel.to_crs("EPSG:6341")
In [6]:
import laspy 
las = laspy.read("USGS_LPC_WY_Southwest_2020_D20_12TWM350530.laz")
print("Point count:", las.header.point_count)
print("Bounds:", las.header.mins, "to", las.header.maxs)
print("Point format:", las.header.point_format)
print("Dimensions:", las.point_format.dimension_names)
print("LAZ CRS:", las.header.parse_crs())

In [7]:
# combine all polygons in gdf into one geometry
clip_geom = parcel.union_all()

# boolean mask for points inside polygon
mask = shapely.contains_xy(clip_geom, las.x, las.y)

clipped_las = laspy.LasData(las.header, points=las.points[mask])
clipped_las.write("clipped_output.laz")
In [8]:
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

fig, ax = plt.subplots()

parcel.plot(ax=ax, facecolor="none", edgecolor="red")

sc = ax.scatter(clipped_las.x, clipped_las.y, c=clipped_las.z, s=1)

plt.colorbar(sc, ax=ax, label="Elevation (m)")
ax.set_title("Clipped LAZ")
ax.set_xlabel("X")
ax.set_ylabel("Y")

plt.show()

import numpy as np
# check that we have correct classifications
print(np.unique(clipped_las.classification))
ground_mask = clipped_las.classification == 2

[1 2 7]
In [10]:
import numpy as np
from scipy.interpolate import griddata
import rasterio
from rasterio.transform import from_origin

# 1) keep only ground points
ground_mask = clipped_las.classification == 2

x = clipped_las.x[ground_mask]
y = clipped_las.y[ground_mask]
z = clipped_las.z[ground_mask]

print("Number of ground points:", len(z))

# 2) choose raster cell size
cell_size = 1.0  # meters

# 3) define raster extent
xmin, xmax = x.min(), x.max()
ymin, ymax = y.min(), y.max()

# 4) make raster cell centers
x_coords = np.arange(xmin, xmax, cell_size)
y_coords = np.arange(ymin, ymax, cell_size)

grid_x, grid_y = np.meshgrid(x_coords, y_coords)

# 5) interpolate directly from points to grid
points = np.column_stack((x, y))
dem = griddata(points, z, (grid_x, grid_y), method="linear")

# 6) flip vertically so row 0 is the top of the raster
dem = np.flipud(dem)

# 7) build affine transform
transform = from_origin(x_coords[0], y_coords[-1] + cell_size, cell_size, cell_size)

# 8) write GeoTIFF
with rasterio.open(
    "parcel_dem_interpolation.tif",
    "w",
    driver="GTiff",
    height=dem.shape[0],
    width=dem.shape[1],
    count=1,
    dtype=dem.dtype,
    crs=clipped_las.header.parse_crs(),
    transform=transform,
    nodata=np.nan
) as dst:
    dst.write(dem, 1)

Number of ground points: 113419
In [11]:
import rasterio
from rasterio.mask import mask

with rasterio.open("parcel_dem_interpolation.tif") as src:
    dem_clipped, transform = mask(src, [clip_geom], crop=True)
    meta = src.meta.copy()

meta.update(
    height=dem_clipped.shape[1],
    width=dem_clipped.shape[2],
    transform=transform
)

with rasterio.open("parcel_dem_clipped.tif", "w", **meta) as dst:
    dst.write(dem_clipped)
In [12]:
import rasterio
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import numpy as np

with rasterio.open("parcel_dem_clipped.tif") as src:
    dem = src.read(1)
    bounds = src.bounds

# mask NaNs so they show as white
dem_masked = np.ma.masked_invalid(dem)

fig, ax = plt.subplots()

cmap = plt.cm.terrain
cmap.set_bad(color='white')

# IMPORTANT: add extent + origin
im = ax.imshow(
    dem_masked,
    cmap=cmap,
    extent=[bounds.left, bounds.right, bounds.bottom, bounds.top],
    origin="upper"
)

# overlay parcel
parcel.plot(ax=ax, facecolor='none', edgecolor='red')

cbar = plt.colorbar(im, ax=ax)
cbar.set_label("Elevation (meters)")

ax.set_title("DEM with Parcel")
ax.set_xlabel("X")
ax.set_ylabel("Y")

plt.show()
In [13]:
import rasterio
import richdem as rd

# read DEM
with rasterio.open("parcel_dem_clipped.tif") as src:
    dem = src.read(1)
    profile = src.profile

# convert to RichDEM format
rd_dem = rd.rdarray(dem, no_data=-9999)

# compute slope (in degrees)
slope = rd.TerrainAttribute(rd_dem, attrib='slope_degrees')

# write slope raster
profile.update(dtype=rasterio.float32, nodata=-9999)

with rasterio.open("parcel_slope.tif", "w", **profile) as dst:
    dst.write(slope.astype("float32"), 1)

Warning! No geotransform defined. Choosing a standard one! (Top left cell's top let corner at <0,0>; cells are 1x1.)
Warning! No geotransform defined. Choosing a standard one! (Top left cell's top let corner at <0,0>; cells are 1x1.)
In [14]:
import rasterio
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import numpy as np

with rasterio.open("parcel_slope.tif") as src:
    slope = src.read(1)
    bounds = src.bounds

# mask NoData if any
slope_masked = np.ma.masked_invalid(slope)

fig, ax = plt.subplots()

im = ax.imshow(
    slope_masked,
    cmap="viridis",
    extent=[bounds.left, bounds.right, bounds.bottom, bounds.top],
    origin="upper"
)

cbar = plt.colorbar(im, ax=ax)
cbar.set_label("Slope (degrees)")

ax.set_title("Slope")
ax.set_xlabel("X")
ax.set_ylabel("Y")

plt.show()
In [15]:
import numpy as np
import geopandas as gpd
from shapely.geometry import box

site_size = 10
step = 5

xmin, ymin, xmax, ymax = parcel.total_bounds

polygons = []
for x in np.arange(xmin, xmax - site_size + step, step):
    for y in np.arange(ymin, ymax - site_size + step, step):
        polygons.append(box(x, y, x + site_size, y + site_size))

grid = gpd.GeoDataFrame(geometry=polygons, crs=parcel.crs)

# merge the parcel into one geometry
parcel_single = gpd.GeoDataFrame(geometry=[parcel.union_all()], crs=parcel.crs)

# clip grid to the merged parcel
grid = gpd.overlay(grid, parcel_single, how="intersection")

stats = zonal_stats(grid, "parcel_slope.tif", stats="mean")

grid["mean_slope"] = [s["mean"] for s in stats]

low_slope = grid["mean_slope"] < 12

grid[low_slope].plot(ax=ax, color="green", edgecolor="black")

ax.set_title("Low Slope Areas (<12°)")
plt.show()
In [22]:
import numpy as np
from scipy.interpolate import griddata
import rasterio
from rasterio.transform import from_origin

# 1) keep only ground points from FULL LAZ
ground_mask = las.classification == 2

x = las.x[ground_mask]
y = las.y[ground_mask]
z = las.z[ground_mask]


# 2) choose raster cell size
cell_size = 1.0  # meters

# 3) define raster extent
xmin, xmax = x.min(), x.max()
ymin, ymax = y.min(), y.max()

# 4) make raster cell centers
x_coords = np.arange(xmin, xmax, cell_size)
y_coords = np.arange(ymin, ymax, cell_size)

grid_x, grid_y = np.meshgrid(x_coords, y_coords)

# 5) interpolate directly from points to grid
points = np.column_stack((x, y))
dem = griddata(points, z, (grid_x, grid_y), method="linear")

# 6) flip vertically so row 0 is the top of the raster
dem = np.flipud(dem)

# 7) build affine transform
transform = from_origin(x_coords[0], y_coords[-1] + cell_size, cell_size, cell_size)

# 8) write GeoTIFF
with rasterio.open(
    "full_dem_interpolation.tif",
    "w",
    driver="GTiff",
    height=dem.shape[0],
    width=dem.shape[1],
    count=1,
    dtype=dem.dtype,
    crs=las.header.parse_crs(),
    transform=transform,
    nodata=np.nan
) as dst:
    dst.write(dem, 1)
In [23]:
import rasterio
import richdem as rd

# read clipped DEM
with rasterio.open("full_dem_interpolation.tif") as src:
    dem = src.read(1)
    profile = src.profile

# convert to RichDEM
rd_dem = rd.rdarray(dem, no_data=-9999)

# fill depressions (important before flow)
rd.FillDepressions(rd_dem, in_place=True)

# compute flow accumulation
flow_acc = rd.FlowAccumulation(rd_dem, method='D8')

profile.update(dtype=rasterio.float32, nodata=-9999)

with rasterio.open("full_dem_flow.tif", "w", **profile) as dst:
    dst.write(flow_acc.astype("float32"), 1)

Warning! No geotransform defined. Choosing a standard one! (Top left cell's top let corner at <0,0>; cells are 1x1.)
Warning! No geotransform defined. Choosing a standard one! (Top left cell's top let corner at <0,0>; cells are 1x1.)

In [24]:
import rasterio
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import numpy as np

with rasterio.open("full_dem_flow.tif") as src:
    flow = src.read(1)
    bounds = src.bounds

flow_masked = np.ma.masked_invalid(flow)

fig, ax = plt.subplots()

# log scale helps visualize flow accumulation
im = ax.imshow(
    np.log1p(flow_masked),
    cmap="Blues",
    extent=[bounds.left, bounds.right, bounds.bottom, bounds.top],
    origin="upper"
)

cbar = plt.colorbar(im, ax=ax)
cbar.set_label("Log Flow Accumulation")

ax.set_title("Flow Accumulation")
ax.set_xlabel("X")
ax.set_ylabel("Y")

plt.show()
In [25]:
import rasterio
from rasterio.mask import mask

# merge parcel to one geometry
clip_geom = [parcel.union_all()]

with rasterio.open("full_dem_flow.tif") as src:
    flow_clipped, transform = mask(src, clip_geom, crop=True)
    meta = src.meta.copy()

meta.update({
    "height": flow_clipped.shape[1],
    "width": flow_clipped.shape[2],
    "transform": transform
})

with rasterio.open("parcel_flow_acc_clipped.tif", "w", **meta) as dst:
    dst.write(flow_clipped)

    np.log1p(flow_masked),
    cmap="Blues",

    origin="upper"
)

cbar.set_label("Log Flow Accumulation")

ax.set_title("Flow Accumulation (Clipped)")

ax.set_ylabel("Y")

plt.show()

In [27]:
from rasterstats import zonal_stats
import numpy as np

stats = zonal_stats(grid, "parcel_flow_acc_clipped.tif", stats="max")
grid["max_flow"] = [s["max"] for s in stats]
grid["log_max_flow"] = np.log1p(grid["max_flow"])

import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

# combined condition
suitable = (grid["log_max_flow"] < 5) & (grid["mean_slope"] < 15)

fig, ax = plt.subplots()

grid[suitable].plot(ax=ax, color="green", edgecolor="black")

ax.set_title("Suitable Sites (Flow < 5 & Slope < 15°)")
plt.show()
